revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:布都御魂]][[zh:布都御魂]]
{{Infobox deity}}


 



{{Nihongo|{{ill|Futsu-no-mitama|ja|布都御魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsu-no-mitama|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|[[:ja:布都御魂|布都御魂]]}} is a [[kami]] in [[Shinto]] and also a {{ill|Sword|en|Sword|qid=Q12791|lt=Sword|1=Sword}}.<ref name="chamberlin-cross-sword" />This sword is the main dedication ([[Shintai|Goshintai]]) kept at [[Isonokami Jingu|Isonokami Shrine]].<ref name="kojiki-takakuraji" />

He is the sword of [[Takemikazuchi]].<ref name="chamberlin-cross-sword" />

His other names include {{Nihongo|Saji-futsu-no-kami|佐士布都神}}, and {{Nihongo|Mika-futsu no kami|甕布都神}}.<ref name="chamberlin-cross-sword" />

The sword aided {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|qid=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}} in his subjugation of the east. At [[Kumano Region|Kumano]], the Emperor and his troops were either struck unconscious by the appearance of a bear (''Kojiki'')<ref name="kojiki-takakuraji">{{Harvnb|武田|1996}}『古事記』text p. 77-8/ mod. Ja. tr. p.260-1</ref><ref name="chamberlin-cross-sword">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|1919|loc=§XLV.—Emperor Jim-mu (Part II.—The Cross-Sword Sent Down From Heaven)., pp.164-}}</ref> or severely debilitated by the poison fumes spewed out by local gods (''Nihon Shoki'').<ref name="aston-birth2">{{Harvnb|Aston|1896|pp=114-115}}</ref><ref name="nihongi-takakuraji">{{Harvnb|宇治谷|1988}}, p.94-5</ref>

But a man named {{ill|Takakuraji|en|Takakuraji|qid=Q11668757|lt=Takakuraji|1=Takakuraji}} presented a gift of a sword, the emperor awoke, and without him hardly brandishing this weapon, the evil deities of Kumano were spontaneously cut down. When Jimmu inquired, Takkuraji explained that he had a vision in a dream where the supreme deities [[Amaterasu]] and [[Takamimusubi|Takamusubi]] were about to command Takemikazuchi to descend to earth once again to pacify the lands, this time to assist the emperor. However, Takemizuchi replied that it would be sufficient to send down the sword he used during his campaigns, and, boring a hole through Takakuraji's storehouse, deposited the sword, bidding the man to present it to Emperor Jimmu.<ref name="chamberlin-cross-sword" /> 

==References==
{{reflist}}
{{Commons Category}}
{{Refbegin}}
*{{cite book|author-link=Basil Hall Chamberlain|last=Chamberlain|first=Basil Hall|title=A Translation of the "Ko-ji-ki," or a record of ancient matters|orig-year=1882|year=1919|url=http://www.sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/index.htm|series=Transactions of the Asiatic Society of Japan|volume=X}}
**{{cite book|last=武田|first=祐吉 (Yūkichi Takeda)|editor=中村啓信|title=新訂古事記|publisher=講談社|orig-year=1977|year=1996|isbn=4-04-400101-4|pages=60, 62, 77, 78, 95}}
*{{cite book|last=Aston|first=William George|title=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697|volume=1|place=London|publisher=Japan Society of London|year=1896|isbn=9780524053478 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=1IJrNAKBpycC&pg=PA299|author-link=William George Aston}}, English translation
**{{cite book|last=宇治谷|first=孟 (Tsutomu Ujitani)|title=日本書紀|volume=上|publisher=講談社|year=1988|isbn=9780802150585}}
{{Refend}}

If I get anywhere near implementing it, I will do so based on the actual completion tiers of this wiki rather than whether I added them when they were on simple english wikipedia or wherever--><!--Futsu-no-mitama--><!--this page is not from a simple english wikipedia draft.
